rules.exact_text: For prescription medicines the invented name, the strength and the pharmaceutical form followed by the common name of the active(s) relevant to the strength in the name should appear in that order, as a cohesive unit and should not be separated by any interpolated text.
rules.reference: HPRA Guide to Labels and Leaflets of Human Medicines (AUT-G0034-26), section 5

rules.exact_text: Where a company chooses this option for an OTC or general sale product, the entire text in italics above will be considered to be the product name. This product name must be listed as an integrated unit in section 1 of the SmPC, on all labels and leaflets and on all advertising related to that product.

rules.exact_text: Article 54 of the Directive requires that space shall be provided on the labelling for the prescribed dose to be indicated.

rules.exact_text: Each individual blister should preferably include the minimum label text for blisters: the name of the product, strength and pharmaceutical form, followed by the common name of the active substance, the name of the PA holder, the expiry date and batch number.
rules.reference: HPRA Guide to Labels and Leaflets of Human Medicines (AUT-G0034-26), section 6.2

rules.exact_text: Directive 2001/83/EC, Article 63(1) permits the use of multilingual text with the proviso that the same information appears in all the languages used.
rules.reference: HPRA Guide to Labels and Leaflets of Human Medicines (AUT-G0034-26), section 6.3; Directive 2001/83/EC Article 63(1)

rules.exact_text: Directive 2001/83/EC, Article 63(1) permits the use of multilingual text with the proviso that the same information appears in all the languages used.

rules.exact_text: A multilingual package must fulfil the following criteria: - The name and the strength of the medicinal product is the same in all languages.
rules.reference: HPRA Guide to Labels and Leaflets of Human Medicines (AUT-G0034-26), section 6.3

rules.exact_text: A multilingual package must fulfil the following criteria: - The name and the strength of the medicinal product is the same in all languages.

HPRA Guide to Labels and Leaflets of Human Medicines: If packaging layout changes, ensure legibility of underlying text beneath Braille (e.g., indications/posology) is not affected
Article 56a, Directive 2001/83/EC (HPRA Guide): MAHs must provide patient information leaflets in formats suitable for blind or partially sighted people (e.g., Braille, audiotape, CD, large print)

HPRA Guide to Labels and Leaflets of Human Medicines: Where no separate patient leaflet exists and required information is on the outer carton, applicant must provide this information in a format suitable for blind or partially sighted patients and submit a Braille declaration accordingly
